question: What's the best time to visit Echuca VIC with kids?
answer: G'day! Spring (September-November) and autumn (March-May) are ripper times to visit Echuca with the little ones. You'll get gorgeous mild weather perfect for paddlesteamer rides and outdoor fun. Summer's great too, especially if your holiday park has a pool, though it can get pretty warm. Winter's quieter and cheaper, but pack the jumpers! School holidays book out fast, so get in early if that's when you're planning to visit.

question: Are there pet-friendly holiday parks in Echuca VIC?
answer: You betcha! Several holiday parks around Echuca welcome your furry family members. Most pet-friendly parks require dogs to be leashed and kept outside cabins, though some allow them in certain accommodation types. You'll usually need to bring your pet's bedding and bowls. Always call ahead to confirm their pet policy and any additional fees. The nearby Murray River trails are fantastic for dog walks too!

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Echuca VIC during school holidays?
answer: During school holidays, expect to pay around $150-$300 per night for a cabin sleeping 4-6 people, depending on the park and cabin type. Powered caravan sites are more budget-friendly at $50-$80 per night. Easter and Christmas holidays are priciest. Book at least 3-6 months ahead for school holiday periods. Off-peak visits can save you 30-40%, so consider sneaking away during term time if possible!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Don't forget the basics: bedding (some parks charge for linen), towels, toiletries, and your kids' favorite pillows. Pack swimmers, sunscreen, hats, and insect repellent. Bring outdoor toys, bikes, or scooters—most parks have heaps of space. Consider portable high chairs if bub needs one. Chuck in some board games for downtime and a basic first-aid kit. Most cabins have kitchens, so BYO pantry staples!

question: What is there to do near Echuca VIC holiday parks for families?
answer: Echuca's absolutely brilliant for families! Take the kids on a paddlesteamer cruise—they'll love it! The Port of Echuca Discovery Centre's interactive, and there's the Great Aussie Beer Shed (mocktails for kids!). Swim at Aquatic Reserve, visit Billabong Ranch for animal encounters, or hire bikes along the Murray. Many holiday parks have playgrounds, jumping pillows, and pools on-site, so there's fun without even leaving!
